name: Pinares de Garrovillas Loop
name_original: Pinares de Garrovillas – circular desde Garrovillas de Alconétar
description: This moderate 16.2-mile circular mountain biking route in Pinares de Garrovillas offers diverse landscapes and views of the Alcántara Reserv
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Garrovillas De Alconétar traverse a landscape characterized by pine forests, open plains, and the proximity to the Tagus River and its reservoirs. The region offers varied terrain, from flat sections ideal for speed to rolling hills with moderate elevation changes. Mountain biking here often involves routes through dehesa landscapes, featuring scattered oak trees and agricultural areas, providing a mix of natural and cultivated scenery. Best mountain bike trails around Garrovillas De Alconétar The most popular mountain bike trail is Return of Pinares de Garrovillas from Garrovillas de Alconétar, a 13.5 miles (21.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the local pine forests with moderate climbs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tour of the Four Places, a moderate 41.2 miles (66.3 km) path. This trail connects several points of interest, offering diverse scenery across the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Pinares de Garrovillas Loop, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Mountain biking around Garrovillas De Alconétar is defined by pine forests, dehesa landscapes, and routes near the Tajo Reservoir. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Garrovillas De Alconétar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Garrovillas De Alconétar?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Garrovillas De Alconétar, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Garrovillas De Alconétar?
A: The trails in Garrovillas De Alconétar cater to various abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 1 difficult trail for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Garrovillas De Alconétar?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy mountain bike trails suitable for families. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Alconétar – Tajo Reservoir loop from Cañaveral&lt;/a&gt;, which is 25.2 km long and offers a gentle ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Garrovillas De Alconétar?
A: The region offers varied terrain, from pine forests and open plains to dehesa landscapes with scattered oak trees and agricultural areas. Many routes also run near the Tagus River and its reservoirs.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for advanced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38143203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Descent by the Lake – Balcón del Mundo loop from Garrovillas de Alconétar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 107.9 km trail with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Garrovillas De Alconétar?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Garrovillas De Alconétar, with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if visiting during this season.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Garrovillas De Alconétar are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014375600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Garrovillas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 26.0 km circular trail through pine forests.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer views of the Tagus River and its reservoirs. You might also encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Tagus Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3789378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alcántara Reservoir and Almonte Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, some routes pass near historical sites. You could explore areas close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3827014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santiago&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5689782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marina of Cañaveral&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of nature and heritage.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Garrovillas De Alconétar?
A: The dehesa landscapes and proximity to the Tagus River mean you might spot various bird species, including raptors, and other local wildlife adapted to the Mediterranean ecosystem.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific cafes directly on trails are not common, many routes start or pass through towns like Garrovillas de Alconétar or Cañaveral, where you can find local establishments for food and drinks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cheese Museum - Torta del Casar&lt;/a&gt; is also a unique stop nearby.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Garrovillas De Alconétar?
A: Most trails originate from or pass through towns and villages such as Garrovillas de Alconétar or Cañaveral, where public parking is generally available. It's advisable to check the specific starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking options.
tips: Start or end your adventure here with a walk, a beer or coffee, or something to eat. The Plaza de la Constitución is the center of the town and is the life of a small town.
It is surprising to find such spectacular ruins. It is a convent dating from 1496. We hope that they can be cared for as they deserve and that the historical heritage that it represents is protected.
The Garrovillas reservoir has been enabled to provide drinking water to the residents of Garrovillas. It is an interesting destination for a walk or a walk.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Pinares de Garrovillas mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered of moderate difficulty for mountain biking. While some initial sections are comfortable and wide, the path can become steeper, with certain areas featuring wooden steps. You might encounter challenging segments where dismounting and pushing your bike is necessary, especially due to sandy dirt roads and granite rocks.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 26 km (16 miles) long and generally takes around 2 hours and 12 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse terrain, starting with pastures, olive groves, and holm oak forests on wide tracks. The route then enters the notable pine forest (Pinar de Garrovillas) with large rock formations, where the path becomes narrower and more winding. Expect undulating sandy dirt roads, paths, and sections with granite rocks.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, including steep sections, wooden steps, and challenging segments where you might need to push your bike, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires good to very good fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pinares de Garrovillas trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Garrovillas de Alconétar. You can typically find parking within the town, for example, near the Plaza de la Constitución, which is a good starting point.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers impressive pine forests, vast rock formations, and expansive steppe lands. You'll pass historical sites like the Convent of San Antonio de Padua and the Shrine of Cristo del Humilladero. From elevated points like Peón, you can enjoy breathtaking views of the pine forest and glimpses of the Garrovillas Reservoir.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: You might spot large groups of vultures flying overhead, as the area is known for its diverse birdlife. The landscape also features holm oak forests and areas abundant with almond trees.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Pinares de Garrovillas trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, and some sections might be less enjoyable. Winters are generally mild but can be wet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for mountain biking in Pinares de Garrovillas?
faqs.answer: The Pinares de Garrovillas area is part of the ZEPA Pinares de Garrovillas, a Special Protection Area for Birds. While there are no specific entrance fees for mountain biking, it's crucial to respect local regulations regarding protected areas, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Pinares de Garrovillas mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially since this area is a ZEPA (Special Protection Area for Birds). Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or unique features to be aware of on this route?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for sections with wooden steps that aid in ascent and areas where you might need to dismount and push your bike due to challenging terrain. The trail also overlaps with the "Ruta de los Almendros" and features a beekeeping operation atop a hill. Rest areas with tables and benches are available for breaks.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Natural del Tajo and the Cañada Real.
